teams.name: Canada
teams.group_summary: Reached the Round of 16 as co-hosts; beat South Africa 1–0 in the Round of 32, then fell 0-3 to Morocco in the Round of 16.
teams.best_wc_finish: Round of 16, 2026 (their best-ever finish — before this tournament Canada had never won a game at a World Cup)
teams.wc_history: Canada had only played two World Cups before this one (1986 and 2022) and lost every single game. As co-hosts in 2026 they finally broke through, winning matches and reaching the knockout rounds for the first time.
teams.storyline: This is the greatest run in Canadian men's soccer history, and it is happening at home. A young, fast team built around superstar left back Alphonso Davies is playing with house money and a nation suddenly paying attention.
teams.style: Quick and direct — they defend in numbers and then break at speed down the wings, especially through Davies.
fun_facts: Before 2026, Canada's men had never scored more than one goal in a World Cup and had never won a match.
Canada is co-hosting this World Cup alongside the USA and Mexico.

teams.name: Morocco
teams.group_summary: Beat the Netherlands on penalties (1–1, 3–2) in the Round of 32 to reach the last 16.
teams.best_wc_finish: Fourth place (2022) — the first African or Arab nation to reach a World Cup semifinal
teams.wc_history: Morocco made history in 2022 by beating Spain and Portugal on their way to the semifinals, farther than any African team had ever gone. They arrive in 2026 no longer as a surprise but as a team everyone respects.
teams.storyline: Morocco want to prove 2022 was no fluke. Backed by huge, loud traveling support wherever they play, they are trying to become the first African nation to reach a World Cup final.
teams.style: Superbly organized defensively — they sit compact, frustrate you, then hit you on the counter with the pace of Achraf Hakimi flying forward.
fun_facts: In 2022 Morocco became the first African or Arab team ever to reach a World Cup semifinal.
Their fans are known for creating some of the loudest atmospheres of any team at the tournament.

teams.name: Paraguay
teams.group_summary: Upset Germany on penalties (1–1, 4–3) in the Round of 32 to reach the last 16.
teams.wc_history: Paraguay are one of South America's tough, stubborn sides. Their best World Cup run came in 2010, when they reached the quarterfinals before losing narrowly to eventual champions Spain.
teams.storyline: Nobody expected Paraguay to still be here. After knocking out Germany on penalties, this defensive, hard-working team is the tournament's great survivor, happy to be underestimated.
teams.style: Defend first, defend deep, and make every game a grind — then pounce on set pieces and counterattacks.
fun_facts: Paraguay knocked out four-time champions Germany on penalties to reach the Round of 16.
They are famous for being one of the hardest teams in the world to score against.

teams.name: France
teams.group_summary: Cruised past Sweden 3–0 in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: France are one of the sport's giants — winners in 1998 and 2018, and runners-up after a classic final in 2022. They almost always arrive as favorites, stacked with talent from front to back.
teams.storyline: Captain and superstar Kylian Mbappé is chasing the one prize that keeps eluding him as the main man: a World Cup as France's leader. This is a deep, dangerous squad expected to go far.
teams.style: Fast, powerful, and clinical — soak up pressure, then break at frightening speed through Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé.
fun_facts: France reached the final in 2022, losing to Argentina on penalties after a 3–3 thriller in which Mbappé scored a hat-trick.
They are one of only a handful of nations to win the World Cup more than once.

teams.name: Brazil
teams.group_summary: Beat Japan 2–1 in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: Brazil are the most successful team in World Cup history with five titles, and the only nation to play at every World Cup ever held. But they haven't won since 2002 — an eternity by their standards.
teams.storyline: The pressure is always the same for Brazil: win, and win beautifully. A new generation led by Vinícius Júnior and Raphinha is trying to end a 24-year title drought that the whole country feels.
teams.style: Flair and pace out wide — Brazil love to attack through skillful wingers who dribble, combine, and shoot.
fun_facts: Brazil is the only country to have played in every World Cup since the tournament began in 1930.
Their five titles are more than any other nation.

teams.name: Norway
teams.group_summary: Beat Ivory Coast 2–1 in the Round of 32 on their long-awaited return to the World Cup.
teams.wc_history: Norway had not qualified for a World Cup since 1998 — a 28-year wait. They finally returned in 2026 carrying one of the most feared strikers on the planet.
teams.storyline: Norway are back on the biggest stage for the first time in a generation, and they have a genuine superstar in Erling Haaland. For a small football nation, simply reaching the knockouts is already history — but with Haaland, anything feels possible.
teams.style: Direct and physical — get the ball forward quickly and let Haaland and Ødegaard do the damage.
fun_facts: Before 2026, Norway hadn't played at a World Cup since 1998.
Norway have a quirky record of never losing to Brazil at a World Cup.

teams.name: Mexico
teams.group_summary: Beat Ecuador 2–0 in the Round of 32 as co-hosts.
teams.wc_history: Mexico are World Cup regulars who almost always reach the knockout rounds, but they had famously been eliminated in the Round of 16 seven tournaments in a row before this one. Their best runs, both quarterfinals, came as hosts in 1970 and 1986.
teams.storyline: Co-hosts Mexico are desperate to finally break their Round-of-16 curse — and they get to try in the iconic, roaring Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. The whole nation is behind them.
teams.style: Technical and possession-based, with quick passing and energy from midfield.
fun_facts: Estadio Azteca is the first stadium to host World Cup matches at three different tournaments (1970, 1986, 2026).
Mexico had been knocked out in the Round of 16 in seven straight World Cups before 2026.

teams.name: England
teams.group_summary: Beat DR Congo 2–1 in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: England won the World Cup on home soil in 1966 and have been chasing a second title ever since. They've come agonizingly close recently — a semifinal in 2018 and a quarterfinal in 2022 — but the long wait goes on.
teams.storyline: The 'it's coming home' story never dies. With a golden generation now in its prime — Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham, Bukayo Saka — England feel the weight of ending 60 years of hurt.
teams.style: Solid and controlled, built on a strong spine, with world-class attackers to unlock tight games.
fun_facts: England's only World Cup title came in 1966, when they hosted the tournament.
Harry Kane is England's all-time leading goalscorer.

teams.name: United States
teams.group_summary: Beat Bosnia & Herzegovina 2–0 in the Round of 32 as co-hosts.
teams.wc_history: The USA finished third at the very first World Cup in 1930. In the modern era their high point was the 2002 quarterfinals. As co-hosts in 2026, expectations — and the spotlight — are bigger than ever.
teams.storyline: This is the USA's tournament to make a statement. A talented young 'golden generation' playing at top European clubs is carrying the hopes of a home crowd that's finally falling for soccer.
teams.style: High energy and athletic — press the opponent hard, win the ball back quickly, and attack through Christian Pulisic.
fun_facts: The USA is co-hosting the 2026 World Cup with Canada and Mexico.
Their captain and star, Christian Pulisic, was the most expensive American player in history when he joined Chelsea.

teams.name: Belgium
teams.group_summary: Came from behind to beat Senegal in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: Belgium's 'golden generation' finished third in 2018, their best-ever result. That group is now older, but names like Kevin De Bruyne and Romelu Lukaku still give them serious quality.
teams.storyline: It may be a last dance for Belgium's celebrated golden generation. Experienced, talented, and knowing time is running out, they're chasing the deep run their talent has always promised.
teams.style: Patient and creative — dominate the ball and let Kevin De Bruyne pull the strings with pinpoint passes.
fun_facts: Belgium beat the USA in the Round of 16 in 2014, a night famous for goalkeeper Tim Howard's record 16 saves.
Kevin De Bruyne is regarded as one of the best passers of his generation.

teams.name: Spain
teams.group_summary: Beat Austria in the Round of 32 to reach the last 16.
teams.wc_history: Spain won their only World Cup in 2010 with a beautiful passing style that changed the sport. As the reigning European champions, they arrive in 2026 as one of the very favorites.
teams.storyline: Spain are back to their brilliant best, blending old-school passing mastery with an exciting new wave led by teenage sensation Lamine Yamal. Many consider them the team to beat.
teams.style: Possession, possession, possession — keep the ball, move the opponent around, and pick them apart with quick combinations.
fun_facts: Spain's 2010 title came from a famous style of quick, short passing known as 'tiki-taka'.
Lamine Yamal became a global star while still a teenager.

teams.name: Portugal
teams.group_summary: Beat Croatia in the Round of 32 to reach the last 16.
teams.wc_history: Portugal have produced some of the game's greatest players — Eusébio in the 1960s, Cristiano Ronaldo today — but their only podium finish was third place in 1966. They are still chasing a first World Cup title.
teams.storyline: This is almost certainly Cristiano Ronaldo's last World Cup, and Portugal are loaded with talent around him. It's a golden chance to give one of the game's biggest icons the send-off — and trophy — he's never had.
teams.style: Flexible and technical, packed with creative midfielders and dangerous wingers.
fun_facts: Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time leading goalscorer in men's international football.
Portugal won the European Championship in 2016, but have never won a World Cup.

teams.name: Argentina
teams.group_summary: Survived a 3–2 thriller against Cape Verde in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: Argentina are the reigning world champions, having won a dramatic 2022 final against France. With three titles and the legends of Diego Maradona and now Lionel Messi, they are one of football's royal families.
teams.storyline: The defending champions are led one more time by Lionel Messi, widely called the greatest of all time, in what is surely his final World Cup. They arrive ranked number one in the world and chasing back-to-back titles.
teams.style: Balanced and street-smart — tough to break down, ruthless in attack, and blessed with Messi's genius in the final third.
fun_facts: Argentina are the defending champions, having won the 2022 final against France on penalties.
Lionel Messi is leading the 2026 Golden Boot race for most goals at the tournament.

teams.name: Egypt
teams.group_summary: Beat Australia on penalties (1–1, 4–2) in the Round of 32 for their first-ever World Cup knockout win.
teams.best_wc_finish: Round of 16, 2026 (their best-ever finish — Egypt had never won a knockout match before)
teams.wc_history: Egypt are one of Africa's most storied national teams — record champions of the Africa Cup of Nations — but had never gone beyond the World Cup group stage until 2026. Reaching the last 16 is uncharted, historic territory.
teams.storyline: Egypt are living a dream, powered by superstar Mohamed Salah. After winning their first-ever World Cup knockout game, one of the continent's proudest football nations is finally making a run on the world stage.
teams.style: Compact and counterattacking, built to get the ball to Salah in space as fast as possible.
fun_facts: Egypt have won the Africa Cup of Nations a record seven times.
Their talisman Mohamed Salah is one of the most prolific goalscorers in Premier League history.

teams.name: Switzerland
teams.group_summary: Beat Algeria in the Round of 32 to reach the last 16.
teams.wc_history: Switzerland are one of the most reliable teams in the world — they almost always qualify and almost always reach the knockouts. Their best World Cup runs, three quarterfinals, all came long ago in the 1930s and 1950s.
teams.storyline: Switzerland are the quiet overachievers again — organized, experienced, and hard to beat. Led by captain Granit Xhaka, they're trying to reach a first World Cup quarterfinal in over 70 years.
teams.style: Disciplined and compact defensively, patient in possession, and dangerous on set pieces.
fun_facts: Switzerland reached the Round of 16 at four of the last five World Cups.
They famously knocked out world champions France on penalties at Euro 2020.

teams.name: Colombia
teams.group_summary: Beat Ghana 1–0 in the Round of 32.
teams.wc_history: Colombia lit up the 2014 World Cup with thrilling, joyful football and a run to the quarterfinals led by James Rodríguez, who won the Golden Boot. They bring that same flair and rhythm in 2026.
teams.storyline: Colombia are one of the tournament's most watchable teams — technical, rhythmic, and fun. With veteran playmaker James Rodríguez orchestrating and Luis Díaz flying down the wing, they believe they can match or beat their 2014 run.
teams.style: Rhythmic and technical — lots of movement, clever passing, and dribbling in the final third.
fun_facts: James Rodríguez won the Golden Boot as top scorer at the 2014 World Cup.
Colombian games are famous for the party atmosphere their fans bring.
